message:
perf(search): encode doc_id with itoa+push_str instead of format! (axwps)

SemanticDocId::to_doc_id_string is a hot-path encoder: once per embedded
message during indexing, once per search hit during semantic lookup. The
prior implementation used format!() twice (hash present / absent) with 7
positional integer args each — format! walks the formatter-trait machinery
per arg and grows its internal buffer on demand.

Replace with a single pre-sized String::with_capacity + itoa::Buffer for
the 7 integer fields. Capacity = 2 ('m|') + 7*20 (max decimal width) + 6
separators + optional 65 for the hash tail. Always fits in one alloc.

Output is byte-identical — verified by the existing parse_semantic_doc_id
roundtrip tests in src/search/vector_index.rs:443 and :467 which encode an
input then parse it back via parse_semantic_doc_id and assert equality.

VERIFICATION:
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane2 cargo test
--lib search::vector_index: 5/5 pass (0.00s).

Closes coding_agent_session_search-axwps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
